key_insight: A locking-free mixed finite element formulation solves elasticity eigenproblems using only the pseudostress tensor, avoiding any symmetry enforcement while still enabling displacement recovery by postprocessing.
engineering_relevance: This offers a robust and scalable route for accurate vibration and stability analysis of elastic structures, especially in regimes where standard FEM can suffer from locking.
method: Mixed FEM built on tensor-valued H(div) finite elements for the pseudostress field
Eigenproblem analyzed with non-compact operator theory to prove convergence and a priori error bounds
A posteriori error estimator developed with proven reliability and efficiency
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence/error analysis and numerical tests across different geometries and physical settings to validate the method.
watch_item: The approach depends on a postprocessed displacement recovery step and specialized mixed tensor elements, so implementation and mesh/space choice may affect practicality.
paper_title: A locking free mixed FEM based on a pure pseudostress based formulation for the elasticity eigenproblem
